title: JWT should be signed and verified with strong cipher algorithms
description: JSON Web Tokens (JWTs), a popular method of securely transmitting information between parties as a JSON object, can become a significant security risk when they are not properly signed with a robust cipher algorithm, left unsigned altogether, or if the signature is not verified. This vulnerability class allows malicious actors to craft fraudulent tokens, effectively impersonating user identities. In essence, the integrity of a JWT hinges on the strength and presence of its signature.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
// Noncompliant: Using weak algorithm
var signingCredentials = new SigningCredentials(key, SecurityAlgorithms.HmacSha1); // Noncompliant
compliant:
// Compliant: Using strong algorithm
var signingCredentials = new SigningCredentials(key, SecurityAlgorithms.HmacSha256); // Compliant

// Compliant: Using RSA-SHA256
var signingCredentials = new SigningCredentials(rsaKey, SecurityAlgorithms.RsaSha256); // Compliant

never_trigger_on: Strong algorithms (HmacSha256, HmacSha384, HmacSha512, RsaSha256, RsaSha384, RsaSha512)
logic.reasoning: Detects JWT tokens with no signature (null) or weak algorithms (None, HmacSha1). JWTs should be signed with strong algorithms like HmacSha256 or RsaSha256. Uses custom function to properly handle method-level test attribute exclusions.
